# LLM Integration Checklist: Non-Stopping Reviewability Markers

**Purpose**: Validate that Claude and Codex autopilot guidance requirements keep reviewability size blocks non-stopping while preserving correctness stops and durable marker evidence.
**Created**: 2026-06-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md) | [plan.md](../plan.md) | [data-model.md](../data-model.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are guidance requirements defined for post-task reviewability `status=block` results so agents continue implementation when the block is valid, current, and size-only? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-002, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK002 Are guidance requirements defined for final full-diff size blocks so agents consume a current marker plan and proceed with `marker_split` instead of stopping for manual re-slicing? [Completeness, Spec §FR-012, Plan §Final Backstop Marker Split API]
- [ ] CHK003 Are correctness-stop instructions preserved for malformed plans, failed verification, invalid PR packets, unsafe output, unusable gate evidence, stale fingerprints, and malformed marker plans? [Completeness, Spec §FR-014]
- [ ] CHK004 Does the plan specify the exact Claude and Codex guidance surfaces whose non-stopping wording must be touched and kept behaviorally equivalent? [Completeness, Plan §Declared File Operations, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK005 Does the spec or plan require future-agent prompts to record the evidence that proves a size-only block became marker-planning input rather than a stop? [Resolved, Spec §FR-018, Plan §LLM Guidance Contract]
- [ ] CHK006 Does the spec or plan require implementation guidance to name the marker-order checkpoint evidence agents must produce after continuing through a size-only block? [Resolved, Spec §FR-017, Spec §FR-018, Plan §LLM Guidance Contract]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK007 Is "size alone" defined clearly enough in guidance requirements to exclude correctness, safety, verification, packet-validation, stale-state, and malformed-evidence failures? [Clarity, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-014, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK008 Is the difference between an automatic marker split and a manual re-slicing stop explicit enough for future agents to avoid asking the operator to re-scope a valid oversized run? [Clarity, Spec §US1, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK009 Are guidance requirements clear that `reviewability-gate.sh tasks` exit code 1 with valid size-only `status=block` JSON is caller-interpreted marker input, not a lower-level contract change? [Clarity, Spec §FR-013, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK010 Are agent-facing instructions required to use one proceed/stop matrix across task reviewability, final backstop, and PR emission guidance? [Clarity, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK011 Does the spec or plan explicitly ban or constrain guidance wording that would reintroduce "stop and manually re-slice" behavior for size-only reviewability blocks? [Resolved, Spec §FR-017, Plan §LLM Guidance Contract]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK012 Are Claude and Codex mirror requirements scoped to behavioral parity rather than byte-for-byte wording, while preserving the same non-stopping rule and correctness-stop boundaries? [Consistency, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK013 Are guidance requirements consistent with the marker-plan state contract so prose prompts cannot become a parallel marker source of truth? [Consistency, Spec §FR-006, Plan §State And Evidence Contract]
- [ ] CHK014 Are marker warnings consistently described as evidence rendered into PR packets after validation, not as a reason to stop implementation? [Consistency, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements, Plan §Error Handling Semantics]
- [ ] CHK015 Are hazard-collapse guidance requirements consistent with non-stopping implementation and marker checkpoint preservation rather than a manual halt? [Consistency, Spec §FR-010]
- [ ] CHK016 Does the plan require mirror parity coverage for the touched Claude and Codex guidance, including the non-stopping size-block rule and final `marker_split` handoff? [Resolved, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-016, Spec §SC-008, Plan §LLM Guidance Contract]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK017 Are guidance requirements defined for valid oversized task output, valid oversized final diff, and hazard-collapsed single-PR emission as separate non-stopping scenarios? [Coverage, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-012]
- [ ] CHK018 Are guidance requirements defined for stale or missing marker plans at final emission so agents stop for correctness instead of inventing review boundaries? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014, Spec §Assumptions]
- [ ] CHK019 Are resume guidance requirements defined so agents preserve marker checkpoints only when marker identity, membership, fold targets, and fingerprints still match? [Coverage, Spec §Assumptions, Plan §State And Evidence Contract]
- [ ] CHK020 Are prompt/evidence requirements defined for future agents to report marker IDs, review order, warnings, fingerprint status, final-backstop result, and packet-validation status after continuing through size-only blocks? [Resolved, Spec §FR-018, Plan §LLM Guidance Contract]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK021 Do success criteria require functional eval coverage that proves future agents continue implementation and emit scoped PRs from marker plans when size is the only negative finding? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-006]
- [ ] CHK022 Do verification requirements cover both Claude guidance and Codex mirror guidance for the same non-stopping reviewability behavior? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-015, Spec §FR-016]
- [ ] CHK023 Are deterministic fixture requirements strong enough to catch guidance or script drift that turns a valid size-only block back into a stop? [Acceptance Criteria, Plan §Contract Fixture Requirements]
- [ ] CHK024 Are success criteria explicit that correctness-stop fixtures still stop in 100% of cases, preventing guidance prompts from over-continuing? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-007]

## Notes

- Items carrying unresolved gap markers require spec.md and/or plan.md remediation before G4 can pass.

## Re-Run Verification

- [ ] CHK025 Does the updated spec require semantic Claude/Codex mirror parity for the same non-stopping size-only block rule, correctness-stop boundaries, final `marker_split` handoff, and evidence fields? [Resolved, Spec §FR-015]
- [ ] CHK026 Does the updated spec require agent-facing guidance to continue implementation for valid size-only reviewability blocks and forbid manual re-slicing stops for size alone? [Resolved, Spec §FR-017]
- [ ] CHK027 Does the updated spec require evidence prompts to record reviewability capture data, marker-plan fingerprint status, ordered markers, checkpoints, warnings, final-backstop result, packet validation, and PR mappings? [Resolved, Spec §FR-018]
- [ ] CHK028 Does the updated plan name the touched Claude and Codex guidance surfaces and define the shared proceed/stop matrix for post-G5, final backstop, hazard collapse, and correctness stops? [Resolved, Plan §LLM Guidance Contract]
- [ ] CHK029 Does the updated plan require paired Claude/Codex eval coverage proving both runtimes continue with marker evidence and do not reintroduce manual re-slicing for size alone? [Resolved, Plan §Contract Fixture Requirements]
